query: Rafah crossing reopened exact date first day February 2026 who was allowed to cross returnees medical cases
a_issues: Minor issue: the featured snippet is dated Feb. 7, 2026 and is not itself the reopening-day report, but it is consistent with the core fact that only five medical patients left on the first day. A does not clearly state in one place how many returnees entered Gaza, though its linked Al Jazeera result mentions a bus carrying 12 Palestinians entering Gaza on the first day.
b_issues: B contains conflicting first-day figures. CGTN says 12 Palestinians entered Gaza and 8 left for medical treatment on the first day, while the Al Jazeera/The Hindu items indicate 12 entered Gaza but Reuters-cited reporting says only a small number of medical patients crossed, and the anchor/reference says five were permitted to leave on day one. This makes B less reliable for the exact first-day medical-case count.
reasoning: The question asks for the exact reopening date/first day in February 2026 and who was allowed to cross, specifically returnees and medical cases. Content A directly includes the strongest matching source: Al Jazeera’s Feb. 2, 2026 report stating the crossing reopened on Monday and that only five Palestinian patients were permitted to leave Gaza on the first day; that same result also mentions a bus carrying 12 Palestinians entering Gaza from Egypt. These facts align with the anchor and are corroborated by AP/Reuters/BBC references shown above the compared sets. Content B does provide useful information about 12 returnees, but it gives an inconsistent count for medical departures (8 instead of 5), creating uncertainty on the exact first-day figure. Therefore A better enables a correct answer.
